subject: Alaska.Ai — LinkedIn Carousel No. 6 — 2026-07-13 — The Interior's Power Math
html_body:
Alaska.Ai · LinkedIn Carousel No. 6

2026-07-13 · 9 slides · branch tsturg/ecstatic-cerf-qngxnc

1 · Upload the document

Download carousel.pdf (3.26 MB, vector text) → LinkedIn → start a post → add a document → upload the PDF → title it: The Interior's Power Math

2 · Paste the post copy

Golden Valley Electric's board weighs $120 million in new gas capacity today, and its members carry the debt.

The turbine would be firm, dated, gas-fired power. The AI data-center loads that make firm capacity feel urgent are still acreage and proposals on Air Force land. Meanwhile GVEA members just absorbed the largest fuel-charge increase in the co-op's history.

Nine slides on what members already paid, what the machine actually buys, and which loads are still on paper. The vote happens in executive session.

Should members finance firm capacity now, or wait until a load actually signs?

#AlaskaEnergy #GVEA #AIDataCenters

3 · Post, then paste this as the FIRST comment (within a minute)

Sources, primary first. The July 13 board vote is an executive-session item, and its outcome was not public at publication.

Fairbanks Daily News-Miner, GVEA board to weigh heavy purchase decision in July.

https://fm.kuac.org/energy-and-the-environment/2026-05-28/gvea-plans-extraordinarily-large-increase-in-co-op-fuel-charge
Alaska's News Source, GVEA rate increase starting June 1.
https://www.alaskasnewssource.com/2026/06/01/gvea-announces-rate-increase-starting-june-1-members-see-higher-bills/
U.S. Air Force, DAF takes steps for potential Alaskan AI data centers.
https://www.af.mil/News/Article-Display/Article/4456660/daf-takes-steps-for-potential-alaskan-ai-data-centers/

https://www.sullivan.senate.gov/newsroom/press-releases/sullivan-secures-56-provisions-historic-26-billion-for-alaska-in-fy-2027-defense-authorization

https://www3.epa.gov/airquality/greenbook/anayo_ak.html

Automation changes this run (1)

The routine modified its own machinery this run. If a later week looks worse, this dated section is the rollback trail (each set reverts as one commit).
KindChangeRollback
fix
engine		render.py's font-loaded QA probe now includes the element's computed font-style, so it tests the FACE ACTUALLY USED: it builds the document.fonts.check() spec as '<style> <weight> 32px "<family>"' (normalizing 'oblique 14deg' -> 'oblique') and keys its per-family dedup on style too, so an italic-only display face is checked against its italic @font-face instead of a hardcoded upright-400; the fonts_missing record and qa.py's FAIL message now carry the style ('font not loaded: <family> w<weight> italic'). Still FAILs a genuinely missing/unloaded declared face; no threshold changed.
why: Recurring false-FAIL (flagged/parked 2026-07-10, touched 2026-07-11, recurred this run): Instrument Serif italic used as the SOFT/accent voice on S3/S6/S7/S9 tripped qa.py 'font not loaded: Instr		revert the upgrade(2026-07-13) commit
pending upgrade(2026-07-13) commit (showrunner stages this set)

Aftercare (this is where reach is won)

  • Post Tuesday to Thursday, 8 to 11 am Alaska time (day signal is the robust one).
  • Paste the first-comment source block within about 60 seconds of posting.
  • Reply substantively to every comment in the first 60 to 90 minutes (golden hour drives reach).
  • The one ask is a debate question; do not stack asks. Weight saves over comments over shares over likes.
  • Optional: cross-post from a personal profile (page carousels carry a reach handicap).
